rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the correct scalar types: int, real, or bool.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference visible AADL features or declared auxiliary variables; avoid undefined or package-qualified identifiers.
Use assign statements in implementation scope only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
For component type contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs without relying on implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Validate that all expressions in guarantees have consistent types; resolve type errors by correcting declarations or removing invalid clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare types explicitly using int, real, or bool; avoid unresolved scalar type references.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model and its dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assign for implementation-level bindings.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Component type annexes and their use for assumptions and guarantees
